t: Smart Lock for French Doors UK
s: Guide to choosing and installing smart locks on UK French doors covering twin cylinder setups, multipoint locking, espagnolette mechanisms, and synchronised access control for double doors.
h1: French Door Smart Lock Compatibility and Options
p1: French doors present unique challenges for smart locking because the active door must lock into the passive door, and the passive door must lock into the frame. In the UK, most French doors use an espagnolette multipoint locking system with a euro-profile cylinder. The primary options are: single smart lock on the active door with a manual shootbolt on the passive door (most common, Yale Conexis L1 Ł180-Ł250 per door), dual smart locks with synchronised app control (Nuki Smart Lock 3.0 Pro Ł250-Ł350 per door, synchronised via app), and full french door smart lock kits (Ultion French Door Kit Ł500-Ł700 from Amazon UK). The active door typically uses a 35/45mm euro cylinder while the passive door uses a shorter 30/30mm. For bifold-style French doors, consider a smart lock on the main active leaf with shootbolts for the remaining panels.
h2: Installation and Synchronisation for French Doors
p2: Step 1: Identify the active and passive leaves. The active leaf has the main handle and lock cylinder. Step 2: Measure the euro cylinder on each leaf separately - they may differ. Step 3: Install the smart lock on the active leaf following standard euro cylinder replacement. Step 4: For the passive leaf, either install a second smart lock or a manual shootbolt. Dual smart locks are recommended for full control. Step 5: If using two smart locks, follow the app pairing process for each. Most apps let you group doors as French Doors to control both simultaneously. Step 6: Configure auto-lock - set 30-second delay. On French doors, ensure both leaves lock together. Step 7: Install a door position sensor on each leaf. Step 8: Test both doors opening independently and together. UK weather conditions cause timber French doors to swell in summer and contract in winter - check alignment seasonally and adjust hinges as needed. Use graphite lubricant on espagnolette mechanisms every 6 months. For insurance purposes, both active and passive leaves should meet TS 007 3-star standard. French doors with single smart lock and manual shootbolt require the passive leaf to be bolted before the active leaf auto-locks. Install a reminder sticker to avoid leaving the passive leaf unsecured.
tb: French Door Solution | Smart Lock Type | Cost per Pair | Insurance Compliant
tr: Single smart lock + manual shootbolt | Yale Conexis L1 or Ultion Smart | Ł250-Ł400 | TS 007 3-star active leaf
Dual smart locks (synchronised) | Nuki Smart Lock 3.0 Pro x2 | Ł500-Ł700 | Full TS 007 3-star both leaves
Full smart French door kit | Ultion French Door Kit | Ł500-Ł700 | Sold Secure Gold
Bifold compatible | Yale Linus Smart Lock | Ł200-Ł300 main leaf | Dependent on shootbolt setup
Smart lock + shootbolt automation | Custom Nuki setup | Ł400-Ł600 | Consult insurer
fq: Can I put a smart lock on only one French door leaf?
Yes - the active leaf gets the smart lock. The passive leaf must be manually shootbolted. Both leaves must be secured for full security.
Do I need two smart locks for French doors?
Not necessarily - one smart lock on the active leaf with a manual shootbolt on the passive leaf works. Dual locks provide full remote control of both leaves.
How do UK seasons affect French door smart locks?
Wooden French doors swell in summer and contract in winter. Check alignment quarterly and adjust hinges to prevent stiff operation or auto-lock failure.
